title: Vast offers new docking adapter for large space stations
summary: Vast introduced a new docking adapter designed for large space stations and spacecraft, offering it to the broader industry. The company aims to support future commercial space station development with this technology.

title: The Digital Networks Act: a reform that requires strategic attention for satellite operators
summary: The Digital Networks Act (DNA) proposed by the European Commission aims to reform the EU's electronic communications framework, potentially impacting spectrum authorization and cross-border service delivery for satellite operators seeking market access.

title: New Wireless Access Point, Camera, and Remote Interface Unit Join NASA Human Rated Class 1 Layer 3 Ethernet Switch Family
summary: Ecliptic Enterprises announced new hardware additions to NASA's Human Rated Class 1 Layer 3 Ethernet Switch Family, including a wireless access point, camera, and remote interface unit. The company, with over 220 missions flown, continues to expand its space avionics and imaging systems portfolio.

title: Turion Space raises $75 million to expand maneuverable satellite fleet
summary: Turion Space has secured $75 million in Series B funding led by Washington Harbour Partners to expand its fleet of maneuverable satellites. The investment will support the development and deployment of advanced satellite technologies.

title: Kepler to lead interoperability tests for ESA’s HydRON optical relay network
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications to lead a hosted payload mission testing terminal interoperability for HydRON, ESA's optical relay network program. This collaboration aims to advance optical communication technologies for space applications.

title: Blue Origin moves toward launch at Vandenberg
summary: Blue Origin is advancing toward launching New Glenn rockets from Vandenberg Space Force Base after the U.S. Space Force decided to finalize lease negotiations for Space Launch Complex-14. The move aims to enhance heavy and super-heavy space launch capabilities at the site.

title: NRO highlights government and industry partnerships
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office is seeking partnerships with government, industry, academia, and allies to enhance its intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities. William Adkins, the NRO's principal deputy director, emphasized the agency's openness to collaboration.

title: SES and Boeing move toward factory-installed multi-orbit inflight connectivity
summary: SES and Boeing have agreed to integrate multi-orbit inflight connectivity hardware during aircraft production, shifting from retrofit installations to include LEO broadband. This collaboration marks a significant advancement in in-flight connectivity technology.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes ispace-U.S. to Board of Directors
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced that ispace-U.S., a U.S.-based lunar exploration company, has joined its Board of Directors. This move highlights the growing collaboration between commercial space entities in advancing lunar exploration efforts.

title: Seagate Space and Oceaneering Join Forces to Build the Future of Offshore Launch Infrastructure
summary: Seagate Space Corporation and Oceaneering International, Inc. have formed a strategic partnership to develop offshore launch platform infrastructure. The collaboration aims to advance Seagate Space's offshore launch platform design through a long-term memorandum of understanding.

title: Solar wind travels up to 4 times faster than expected, eclipse spacecraft reveals
summary: The Solar Orbiter spacecraft observed solar wind gusts in the inner corona moving three to four times faster than expected. This discovery challenges previous understanding of solar wind behavior.

title: See Comet PanSTARRS glow above a false dawn in stunning new photo
summary: A stunning new photo captures Comet PanSTARRS glowing above a false dawn. The comet is already visible to the naked eye ahead of its perihelion passage on April 19.

title: A worst-case solar storm could trigger panic buying and public unrest, report warns
summary: A report warns that a worst-case solar storm could trigger panic buying and public unrest by influencing human behavior. Scientists highlight risks including protests and misinformation.

title: SpaceX launches two Starlink satellite groups 19 hours apart
summary: SpaceX launched two Falcon 9 rockets carrying Starlink satellites within 19 hours, with launches from Florida and California on April 14, 2026.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moonshot was just the 'opening act' for America's return to the moon, space agency chief says
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is seen as the first step in a broader lunar exploration plan, with plans for a moon base and eventual missions to Mars. NASA and its partners are framing the mission as part of a long-term 'relay race' to establish a sustainable human presence on the moon.

title: The 10 best Stargate SG-1 episodes, ranked
summary: A ranking of the top 10 episodes of Stargate SG-1 places Chevron seven at the top. The list highlights the best moments from the popular sci-fi series.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon shot just landed on Saturday Night Live — space toilet jokes and all (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission was parodied on Saturday Night Live with jokes about space toilets, following the successful splashdown of the mission.

title: Scientists use rare 'Einstein Cross' to learn about young galaxy with surprisingly old stars
summary: Scientists utilized a rare 'Einstein Cross' to study a young galaxy with unexpectedly old stars, revealing insights into its early formation. The gravitational lensing effect provided a detailed view of the galaxy's stellar composition.

title: This Artemis 2 astronaut really loves Rise | Space photo of the day for April 14, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 mission features a mascot named 'Rise Wiseman,' which is being celebrated as a beloved figure in space exploration history. The mascot is highlighted in a space photo of the day for April 14, 2026.

title: Contours of the James Bay Lowlands
summary: The retreat of the Laurentide Ice Sheet from present-day Hudson Bay has revealed striking nearshore topography in the James Bay Lowlands. The rebounding land has created distinct geological features.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4859-4866: One Small Crater and Thousands of Polygons
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover recently drove toward a small crater named 'Antofagasta' after a region in Chile. The rover's activities were detailed in a blog post by the Deputy Project Scientist at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

title: NASA Finds Young Stars Dim in X-rays Surprisingly Quickly
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered that young stars similar to the Sun dim in X-ray output faster than expected. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, challenge previous assumptions about stellar activity.

title: NASA Receives 7 Nominations for the 30th Annual Webby Awards
summary: NASA has received seven nominations for the 30th Annual Webby Awards, highlighting its excellence in digital communications and online outreach. The agency's evolution from traditional media to social platforms has been acknowledged for its broad reach.

title: 2025-2026 Dream with Us Design Challenge Winners
summary: The 2025-2026 Dream with Us Design Challenge announced its winners, highlighting projects like Scout Farm, AgriTech, and AgriDrone from middle school participants across the U.S.

title: A Hug for Home Away from Home
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch, part of the Artemis II mission, hugs the Orion spacecraft aboard USS John P. Murtha after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean. The crew, including astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, completed their mission successfully.

title: April 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The April 2026 Satellite Puzzler challenges participants to identify the location and significance of a satellite image. The puzzle aims to engage the public in space imagery interpretation.

title: Nutrition Research Arrives Aboard Space Station
summary: Nutrition research has arrived at the International Space Station to support long-term human missions to the Moon, Mars, and beyond. The focus is on growing plants and algae for sustainable food sources in space.

title: NASA’s Webb Redefines Dividing Line Between Planets, Stars
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has helped astronomers examine 29 Cygni b, an object that challenges traditional planet formation theories. The study aims to understand the dividing line between planets and stars, particularly for massive objects.

title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is expected to hit the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam in mid-April 2026, posing significant risks to the region. The storm's trajectory and intensity are being closely monitored by local authorities and emergency services.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will conduct its third launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program. This follows the successful return of Orion, marking a key step in preparation for future missions.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent NG-23 Cygnus mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at its Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing preparations at SpaceX's Boca Chica facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a record by conducting a lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the ISS, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission approaches its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates key milestones in space exploration and science.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris in June 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona observations. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved navigation or attitude control systems. It highlights innovations that enhance precision in space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials efficiently in microgravity, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment using a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: Researchers have developed a silicon chip capable of performing multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space-based computing. This advancement could enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite data services. This follows a wave of space tech IPOs in the industry.

title: Kepler, Astrolight to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test its 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He emphasizes the importance of balancing scientific passion with practical financial planning.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) companies dispute NASA's assessment, while NASA challenges them to disprove their claims. The disagreement was highlighted during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered platform enabling operators to detect anomalies and make decisions through a unified interface. The platform aims to streamline space operations with advanced artificial intelligence.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has launched as a new business unit focused on working with the Pentagon and NASA. The company is led by a former Texas representative.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast introduced a new flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company aims to modernize astronaut apparel with a stylish yet functional approach.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify the procurement of orbital data centers. This initiative aims to accelerate the adoption of orbital computing by making it more accessible.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital data center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in a real-world orbital environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO, joining other defense-focused space companies seeking public funding. This move aims to provide more capital as the space industry continues to expand.

title: Rocket Lab Closes Mynaric Acquisition at Higher Price Than Originally Agreed
summary: Rocket Lab completed its acquisition of Mynaric AG for $155.3 million, exceeding the original deal terms by $5.3 million. The acquisition focuses on Mynaric's optical communications terminal technology.

title: Allied Space Strategies Stress Complementary Capabilities for Burden Sharing in Orbit
summary: Allied Space Strategies emphasizes the importance of complementary capabilities among allies and partners to enhance collective space domain capabilities, resilience, and deterrence. The focus is on burden sharing to strengthen orbital operations.

title: NASA Administrator Isaacman Hails the Success of Artemis, Previews Plans for Artemis III and IV
summary: NASA Administrator Isaacman praised the success of the Artemis program and outlined plans for future missions, including Artemis III and IV. The announcement highlighted efforts to accelerate the establishment of a lunar base.

title: Karman Director of Space & Launch Market Renee Frohnert Recaps Artemis II Support
summary: Karman Space & Defense's Director of Space & Launch Market, Renee Frohnert, discusses the company's support for Artemis II in an interview with Via Satellite's Managing Editor Rachel Jewett at the Space Symposium.

title: Delta Air Lines Contracts Airbus to Install Hughes Co-Developed IFC on Upcoming A350-1000 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has contracted Airbus to install a multi-orbit in-flight connectivity (IFC) solution co-developed with Hughes on 20 of its upcoming A350-1000 aircraft. The agreement leverages Hughes' satellite technology for enhanced connectivity.

title: Amazon Enters D2D Market With Globalstar Acquisition and Plans for its Own System
summary: Amazon has acquired Globalstar to enter the direct-to-device (D2D) satellite market and plans to deploy its own D2D satellite system.

title: EBAD’s Lisa Brown Talks Supporting Customer Missions at Space Symposium
summary: EBAD highlighted its long-standing support for critical customer missions, including Artemis II, at the Space Symposium. Lisa Brown of EBAD discussed the company's 190-year heritage in the space industry.

title: Lockheed Martin Planning Next-Generation Space Dominance Demos On Orbit
summary: Lockheed Martin is self-funding two next-generation space dominance demonstrations focused on rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) and command. This effort aims to meet the U.S. Defense Department's demand for advanced orbital warfare capabilities.

title: Regulators Challenged With Keeping Up With the Cadence of Space Development
summary: Regulators are struggling to keep up with the rapid pace of commercial space development over the past decade. They are working to establish oversight while enabling innovation in the industry.

title: ThinKom Rolls Out New ThinAir Nexus Multi-Orbit, Multi-System Antenna for Airlines
summary: ThinKom introduced the ThinAir Nexus, a new multi-orbit electronically steered antenna (ESA) designed for aircraft. The California-based satellite ground-systems technology company unveiled the product to enhance connectivity solutions for airlines.
